summary: Unit tests for core logic (retention policies, deletion plan generation) are well-structured with realistic fixtures and good edge case coverage. However, critical gaps exist: (1) NO integration testing via act—task explicitly requires 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT' but workflow.test.ts only validates YAML structure, doesn't execute the workflow; (2) src/main.ts (entry point, argument parsing, file I/O, error handling) is completely untested; (3) no end-to-end testing of the script with actual fixtures; (4) missing error scenario tests (invalid files, malformed JSON, bad arguments). The retention.test.ts and cleanup.test.ts would catch regressions in pure functions, but the test suite would NOT catch failures in the actual CLI, file handling, or CI/CD integration—which are critical for a scripting task that must work in GitHub Actions.
